steps.title: Identify the parallel examples
steps.content: The passage defines ekphrastic poetry, then presents two examples: Hayden's poem about Monet's painting and Pimentel's poem about Klimt's painting. Both demonstrate the same concept — poets exploring visual art through poetry. The transition must signal this parallel relationship, not a contrast, consequence, or reversal.

steps.title: Match the parallel structure with the right transition
steps.content:
Likewise means “in the same way” — it signals that Pimentel's poem follows the same pattern as Hayden's, both engaging with visual art through poetry.
The other choices signal different relationships — contrast (nevertheless, conversely) or causation (hence) — that don't fit parallel examples.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
